channel.title: maGaming RSS Feed - insider-gaming.com
channel.description: A cleaned-up version of the original gaming feed for insider-gaming.com

item.title: Jason Blundell Forms Another New Studio
item.content:encoded:
Dark Outlaw Games has only been shut down for a little over a week, and the former head, Jason Blundell, has already formed another new studio.

During a recent appearance on colleague JCbackfire’s stream, Blundell announced the formation of MagicFractal. Details remain scarce on what exactly the studio is or what it could be working on. And that’s expected with it only just being formed.

Still, with Blundell leading another new studio, there are questions as to why he would do this again. To that point, he was very short and sweet with his answer.

“Third time’s the charm,” he said. “And if not, it’s the fourth time.”

After the closure of Dark Outlaw Games, Blundell gave a little bit of insight into what he was working on. The game, he said, wasn’t a live service title, and that it was something that would have had fans “very excited”.

With MagicFractal, the hope is that Blundell’s luck makes a turn for the better. Since leaving Treyarch in 2020, Blundell has led two studios—Deviation Games and Dark Outlaw Games—, but neither was able to put out a game.

What do you think of Blundell jumping right back in and starting a new studio in MagicFractal? Leave your thoughts in the comments below, and in this official Insider Gaming Discord.

In other news, read Insider Gaming’s exclusive feature on how Virtuos became one of gaming’s biggest studios. And for even more Insider Gaming delivered directly to your inbox, sign up for our newsletter.

Mike has been covering the gaming industry since 2012, and has reported on some of the largest events in the industry while also working as an investigative reporter. Outside of…

More from Mike Straw

item.title: Starfield Features Enhanced Visuals on the PlayStation 5
item.content:encoded:
With the release of Starfield on PlayStation 5 scheduled for April 7, Bethesda has unveiled a new trailer showcasing what PS5 players can expect from the game. Released on April 3, the trailer highlights key features, including adaptive trigger support that enhances weapon resistance using the Sony PlayStation 5 DualSense controller.

Additionally, the controller will alert players to their health status with the light bar around the touchpad. There will also be voice logs from the controller’s speaker.

Starfield PS5 Features Trailer Drops

The trailer also touts upgraded quality and performance modes specifically for the PS5. It’s noted that Starfield on the PS5 offers enhanced visual fidelity in Quality Mode. Also, Performance Mode targets 60 FPS on the platform.

“On PlayStation 5 Pro, you can pick the look and feel that fits your playstyle. Pro Visual Mode delivers a 4K final rendering output targeting 30 FPS for maximum visual fidelity, while Pro Performance Mode targets 60 FPS for a faster, more fluid experience. Both modes are powered by PSSR (PlayStation Spectral Super Resolution) upscaling technology,” says Bethesda.

Speaking of Starfield visuals, the game has recently been used in test runs of NVIDIA’s DLSS 5 technology. Learn more about that here.

Starfield was originally an Xbox exclusive. The game was released on September 6, 2023. Currently, despite Xbox undergoing a rebrand and a supposed shift back to catering to loyal Xbox players, Starfield is among many original Xbox exclusives now being ported to PlayStation.

Learn more about the future of Xbox since Asha Sharma took over at this link.

Are you excited to play Starfield on the PlayStation 5? Sound off in our official Discord server. Also, don’t forget to sign up for our weekly newsletter.
